titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD 1.1.Sacr.12

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=10 status=live modified=2022-10-06 22:26:42.989673+00 | translation moa=9 status=live modified=2023-01-27 15:59:13.83033+00

p.cl: Walterus del Bois Omnibus sancte matris Ecclesie filiis uidentibus uel audientibus has litteras Salutem .

Sciatis me pro salute anime mee. ⁊
Patris mei. ⁊ Matris mee. & Galiene sponse mee. ⁊ Ricardi heredis mei. ⁊ antecessorum meorum. ⁊ successorum meorum. dedisse. ⁊ conces
sisse. ⁊ et hac presenti Carta confirmasse deo et sancto Cuthberto et monachis Dunelmensibus in puram ⁊ perpetuam elemosinam terram illam
apud Tauenaus per istas diuisa. Scilicet. del Redehow super kircelesbanc usque ad uiam que tendit apud Roberdesbi. ⁊ per illam uiam
uersus aquilonem usque ad primum sich quod uenit de petera. ⁊ cadit in kirtel. ⁊ descendendo usque ad redebanc. Quare uolo. ⁊ pre
cipio. quod predicti monachi habeant. ⁊ possidant totam terram predictam infra prenominatas diuisas. in bosco ⁊ plano. cum communi
pastura uille de Carruderes ad Centum bidentes. ⁊ Quadraginta aueria. ⁊ ad Quinquaginta capras. ⁊ viginti porcos. ⁊ ad
tres equos. libere. quiete ⁊ honorifice. ab omni consuetudine uel auxilio. ⁊ exactione. sicut aliqua Elemosina in toto Regno
liberius uel quietius habetur. uel possidetur.

Hiis testibus. Ricardo filio meo . Galiena sponsa mea. Radulfo de Wansant. Padino pre
posito. Willelmo de Ispania. Roberto de Lithum. Adam filio Ricardi. Stephano clerico. Hamone. Gaufrido filio albelot. ⁊ multis aliis.

div.p: Walter del Bois to all sons of holy mother Church who see or hear these letters : Greeting. Let them know that, for the health of my own soul, and of my father’s, and of my mother’s, and of Galiena my spouse’s, and of Richard my son’s, and of my ancestors’, and successors’, I have given, and granted, and by this present Charter have made firm to God and to Saint Cuthbert and the monks of Durham in pure and perpetual alms the land at ‘Tavenaus’ by these boundaries: that is, from the ‘Redehow’ on the ‘Kircelesbanc’ to the road that leads to ‘Roberdesbi’, and by that road northwards to the first syke that comes from the peat bog, and falls into the Kirtle, and by descending as far as ‘Redebanc’. Wherefore I will, and enjoin, that the aforesaid monks should have, and possess the whole land aforesaid within the aforenamed boundaries, in wood and plain, with common pasture of the toun of Carruthers for a hundred sheep, forty cattle, fifty goats, twenty pigs, and three horses, freely, quietly and honourably, from all custom or aid, and exaction, just as any Alms in the whole Kingdom is freely or quietly had, or possessed. With these witnesses : Richard my son; Galiena my spouse; Ralph of Wassand; Páitín the grieve; William d'Epaignes; Robert de Lithum; Adam son of Richard; Stephen the clerk; Hamo; Geoffrey son of Albelot; and many others.
